q: Although it was raining, he went out without an umbrella. (Join the sentences, beginning with 'In spite of')
a: In spite of the rain, he went out without an umbrella.

q: She passed her exams though she didn't study much. (Join the sentences, using 'even though')
a: She passed her exams even though she didn't study much.

q: In spite of his age, he is incredibly active and energetic. (Join the sentences, beginning with 'Despite')
a: Despite his age, he is incredibly active and energetic.

q: He didn't enjoy the movie, though the excellent reviews. (Join the sentences, using 'even though')
a: He didn't enjoy the movie, even though the excellent reviews.

q: Despite the challenges, the team managed to complete the project on time. (Join the sentences, using 'in spite of')
a: In spite of the challenges, the team managed to complete the project on time.
